messages.content:
You are an expert Spanish linguist generating natural, varied example sentences for idiom senses.

Task:
For each sense, generate example sentences in Spanish with English translations.

Rules:
- Generate 3 sentences for idiomatic senses, 4 sentences for literal senses
- Each sentence must embed the idiom naturally with realistic surrounding context
- Context should feel like real speech or writing — include names, situations, backstory where natural.
  For example, rather than just "He let the cat out of the bag", write something like:
  "I told Mark not to say anything, but of course he let the cat out of the bag before I could surprise her."
- Vary morphology across sentences. Explicitly use different forms such as:
  - Different tenses (past, present, future, conditional)
  - Different persons (1st, 2nd, 3rd person)
  - Passive and active voice
  - Negation
  - Question forms
- Sentences should feel natural, not mechanical or repetitive
- Output ONLY valid JSON — no explanations, no markdown
- Format:
[
  {
    "idiom_id": "...",
    "sense_number": 1,
    "examples": [
      {"sentence": "...", "translation": "..."},
      ...
    ]
  }
]

DATA:

en_unspecified_2267 | sense 1 | idiom: ni rey ni roque | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Literal: neither the king nor castling (roque), referencing chess pieces or moves.

en_unspecified_2267 | sense 2 | idiom: ni rey ni roque | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Figurative: neither one thing nor the other; used to say that something does not correspond to either of two alternatives or does not fit any expected category.

en_unspecified_2268 | sense 1 | idiom: ni tanto ni tan calvo | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Literal: neither so much nor so bald; a literal juxtaposition suggesting moderation between two extremes.

en_unspecified_2268 | sense 2 | idiom: ni tanto ni tan calvo | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Figurative: used to advise moderation or to reject exaggerated positions; 'not that extreme nor that negligible', meaning the truth lies somewhere in between or do not overstate things.

en_unspecified_2269 | sense 1 | idiom: niña bonita | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Literal: a young female who is considered physically attractive; a pretty girl.

en_unspecified_2269 | sense 2 | idiom: niña bonita | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Vocative/term of endearment used to address a female with affection or tenderness, equivalent to 'sweetie' or 'pretty girl'.

en_unspecified_2269 | sense 3 | idiom: niña bonita | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Figurative: the person who attracts universal admiration or attention; the favourite or 'darling' of a group.

en_unspecified_2270 | sense 1 | idiom: no atar ni desatar | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Literal: not to tie nor to untie; to neither fasten nor loosen.

en_unspecified_2270 | sense 2 | idiom: no atar ni desatar | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Figurative: to lack authority or influence to make decisions or resolve matters; to be powerless or unable to take action.

en_unspecified_2271 | sense 1 | idiom: no callarse ni bajo el agua | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Figurative: to be unable or unwilling to keep quiet under any circumstances; to talk incessantly or refuse to remain silent.

en_unspecified_2271 | sense 2 | idiom: no callarse ni bajo el agua | type: literal_tentative | sentences: 4
Definition: Literal: not to remain silent even when submerged under water (hyperbolic expression emphasizing persistence in speaking).

en_unspecified_2272 | sense 1 | idiom: no callarse ni debajo del agua | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Figurative: to be incapable or unwilling to keep silent in any situation; to speak continuously or refuse to be quiet.

en_unspecified_2272 | sense 2 | idiom: no callarse ni debajo del agua | type: literal_tentative | sentences: 4
Definition: Literal: not to stay quiet even when under water (a hyperbolic literal rendering used for emphasis).

en_unspecified_2273 | sense 1 | idiom: no casarse con nadie | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Literal: to choose not to marry any person; to remain unmarried.

en_unspecified_2273 | sense 2 | idiom: no casarse con nadie | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Figurative: to avoid committing to a single option, party, opinion, or side; to remain noncommittal or impartial.

en_unspecified_2274 | sense 1 | idiom: no comerse un rosco | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: To not eat a rosco (a ring-shaped pastry).

en_unspecified_2274 | sense 2 | idiom: no comerse un rosco | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: To obtain nothing; to fail to achieve any result.

en_unspecified_2275 | sense 1 | idiom: no dar palo al agua | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Literally, to not put or give a stick to the water.

en_unspecified_2275 | sense 2 | idiom: no dar palo al agua | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: To be idle; to avoid doing any work or effort.

en_unspecified_2276 | sense 1 | idiom: no dar pie con bola | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Literally, to fail to make the foot meet the ball (as in failing to connect with the ball).

en_unspecified_2276 | sense 2 | idiom: no dar pie con bola | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: To be unable to do anything correctly; to be repeatedly unsuccessful or awkward.

en_unspecified_2277 | sense 1 | idiom: no dar un palo al agua | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Literally, to not give or put a stick into the water.

en_unspecified_2277 | sense 2 | idiom: no dar un palo al agua | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: To be lazy; to refuse to do any work or to make no effort.

en_unspecified_2278 | sense 1 | idiom: no dar una a derechas | type: literal_tentative | sentences: 4
Definition: Literally, to not give a single one to the right.

en_unspecified_2278 | sense 2 | idiom: no dar una a derechas | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: To be unable to do anything correctly; to fail continually or be completely inept.

en_unspecified_2279 | sense 1 | idiom: no decir ni mu | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Literal: not to utter even the smallest sound (mu as an onomatopoeic minimal sound).

en_unspecified_2279 | sense 2 | idiom: no decir ni mu | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Figurative: to remain completely silent; not to say a word.

en_unspecified_2280 | sense 1 | idiom: no decir ni pío | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Literal: not to make even a peep or chirping sound (pío as the onomatopoeic bird sound).

en_unspecified_2280 | sense 2 | idiom: no decir ni pío | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Figurative: to keep silent; not to divulge information or say anything at all.

en_unspecified_2281 | sense 1 | idiom: no decir palabra | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Literal: not to pronounce any word.
